28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

465	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

116	def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
117	    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False
118	
119	    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
120	    sentencepiece_model = False
121	    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
122	    created_folder = False
123	    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
124	        created_folder = True
125	        os.makedirs(file_location)
126	    pass
127	    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
128	    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
129	        sentencepiece_model = True
130	    pass
131	    if created_folder:
132	        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
133	    return sentencepiece_model

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass
156	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

734	def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
735	    dtype = X.dtype
736	    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
737	
738	    if X.dim() == 3:
739	        batch, seq_len, d = X.shape
740	        X = X.view(-1, X.shape[-1])
741	        reshape = True
742	    else:
743	        reshape = False
744	    pass
745	    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
746	    if W_quant is not None: del W
747	
748	    if A is not None:
749	        # LoRA is enabled
750	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
751	        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
752	        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)
753	        # out += (X @ A.to(dtype)) @ (s * B.to(dtype))
754	    pass
755	
756	    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out

2502	def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
2503	    self,
2504	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2505	    tokenizer            = None,
2506	    torchao_config       = None,
2507	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2508	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2509	):
2510	    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint
2511	
2512	    Args
2513	      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
2515	      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
2516	    """
2517	    # first merge the lora weights
2518	    arguments = dict(locals())
2519	    arguments["model"]        = self
2520	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
2521	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
2522	    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2523	    del arguments["self"]
2524	    del arguments["torchao_config"]
2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2526	    for _ in range(3):
2527	        gc.collect()
2528	
2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:
2532	        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
2533	        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
2534	    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)
2535	
2536	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
2537	    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
2538	        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
2539	    else:
2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
2542	        arguments["save_directory"],
2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549	
2550	    if push_to_hub:
2551	        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
2552	        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
2553	        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
2554	        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
2555	    else:
2556	        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
2557	        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
2558	    pass
2559	    for _ in range(3):
2560	        gc.collect()
2561	pass

72	def try_fix_tokenizer(tokenizer, prepend = True):
73	
74	    if hasattr(tokenizer, "_tokenizer"):
75	        converted_tokenizer = tokenizer._tokenizer
76	    else:
77	        converted_tokenizer = convert_slow_tokenizer(tokenizer)
78	    pass
79	
80	    tokenizer_string = converted_tokenizer.to_str()
81	
82	    # Llama does _apple. Sometimes this is wrong!!
83	    prepend_text = '{"type":"Prepend","prepend":"▁"},'
84	    if not prepend and prepend_text in tokenizer_string:
85	        tokenizer_string = tokenizer_string.replace(prepend_text, "", 1)
86	    pass
87	
88	    dir_names = dir(tokenizer)
89	    # Get eos_token, bos_token etc
90	    token_names = [x for x in dir_names if x.endswith("_token") and x.count("_") == 1]
91	
92	    for token_name in token_names:
93	        token = getattr(tokenizer, token_name, None)
94	        if token is None: continue
95	        token_id = getattr(tokenizer, token_name + "_id", None)
96	
97	        # Locate the token's id mapping in the string
98	        find_text = f'"id":{token_id},"content":"'
99	        start = tokenizer_string.find(find_text) + len(find_text)
100	        if start == -1: continue
101	        end   = tokenizer_string.find('",', start)
102	
103	        bad_token = tokenizer_string[start : end]
104	        # Check if token is the actual same one - if not, edit it
105	        if bad_token != token:
106	            bad_text  = f'{find_text}{bad_token}",'
107	            good_text = f'{find_text}{token}",'
108	            tokenizer_string = tokenizer_string.replace(bad_text, good_text, 1)
109	
110	            # And replace vocab section
111	            bad_text = f'"{bad_token}":{token_id},'
112	            good_text = f'"{token}":{token_id},'
113	            tokenizer_string = tokenizer_string.replace(bad_text, good_text, 1)
114	        pass
115	    pass
116	
117	    fixed_tokenizer = converted_tokenizer.from_str(tokenizer_string)
118	    return fixed_tokenizer
119	pass

122	def get_sorted_dict(dictionary):
123	    sorted_keys = sorted(dictionary.values())
124	    inverted_dictionary = { value : key for key, value in dictionary.items() }
125	
126	    sorted_dictionary = {}
127	    for key in sorted_keys:
128	        value = inverted_dictionary[key]
129	        sorted_dictionary[value] = key
130	    return sorted_dictionary

137	):
138	    is_fast = getattr(slow_tokenizer, "is_fast", False)

143	        lowered_tokenizer_name = tokenizer_name.lower()

146	            FastTokenizer = eval(
147	                f'__import__(f"transformers.models.{class_name}").{tokenizer_name}Fast'
148	            )

150	            FastTokenizer = PreTrainedTokenizerFast

25	def search_models(org: str = None, base_name: str = None, version: str = None, size: str = None, quant_types: list[QuantType] = None, search_pattern: str = None) -> list[ModelInfo]:
26	    """
27	    Get model info from the registry.
28	
29	    See registry.ModelInfo for more fields.
30	
31	    If search_pattern is provided, the full model path will be matched against the pattern, where the model path is the model_id on huggingface hub.
32	
33	    """
34	    if not _ARE_MODELS_REGISTERED:
35	        register_models()
36	    
37	    model_infos = MODEL_REGISTRY.values()
38	    if org:
39	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.org == org]
40	    if base_name:
41	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.base_name == base_name]
42	    if version:
43	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.version == version]
44	    if size:
45	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if model_info.size == size]
46	    if quant_types:
47	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if any(model_info.quant_type == quant_type for quant_type in quant_types)]
48	    if search_pattern:
49	        model_infos = [model_info for model_info in model_infos if search_pattern in model_info.model_path]
50	    
51	    return model_infos

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive analysis document:

## Unsloth Package Structure Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the unsloth Python package. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of its structure and how it works:

### **1. Top-Level Package Structure**

**Main Directory:** `/unsloth/`

**Key Files:**
- **`__init__.py`** (11KB) - Main package entry point
- **`save.py`** (105KB) - Model saving/export functionality (GGUF, merged, etc.)
- **`tokenizer_utils.py`** (41KB) - Tokenizer utilities and fixes
- **`chat_templates.py`** (129KB) - Chat template management
- **`trainer.py`** (8KB) - Trainer integrations
- **`import_fixes.py`** (6KB) - Import-time patches for dependencies

**Directories:**
- `models/` - Model-specific implementations
- `kernels/` - CUDA/GPU optimization kernels
- `registry/` - Model registry system
- `dataprep/` - Data preparation utilities
- `utils/` - Utility functions

---

### **2. How Models Are Patched - The Key Mechanism**

**The monkey-patching happens in `save.py` via the `patch_saving_functions()` function (lines 2569-2668):**

This is where methods like `save_pretrained_merged` are dynamically attached to model instances:

```python
# From save.py lines 2657-2668
# Add saving methods to top level model
if not vision:
    if hasattr(model, "config"):
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
        model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
        model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**Key Functions in save.py:**
- `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (lines 2413-2453) - Main merged save function
- `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (lines 2456-2499) - Push merged model to Hub
- `patch_saving_functions()` (lines 2569+) - Attaches all save methods using `types.MethodType()`

---

### **3. FastLanguageModel Class**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/loader.py` (lines 107+)

**Class Hierarchy:**
```
FastLanguageModel (inherits from FastLlamaModel)
  ├── from_pretrained() - Static method to load models
  └── get_peft_model() - Static method to apply LoRA
```

**Key Methods:**
- **`from_pretrained()`** (line 109) - Loads a pretrained model with optimizations
  - Returns: `(model, tokenizer)` tuple
  - Calls `patch_saving_functions(model)` at the end (line 2204 in llama.py)
  - Calls `patch_saving_functions(tokenizer)` for the tokenizer (line 2188 in llama.py)

- **`get_peft_model()`** (line 2266 in llama.py) - Applies LoRA to a model
  - Configures LoRA parameters (rank, alpha, dropout, target modules)
  - Returns modified model ready for training

**The Patching Flow:**
1. User calls `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(...)`
2. Model is loaded and optimized
3. `patch_saving_functions(model)` is called (line 2204 in llama.py)
4. Save methods are attached via `types.MethodType()`
5. Model is returned with new methods available

---

### **4. Tokenizer Utilities**

**Location:** `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` (41KB)

**Exported Functions:**
```python
__all__ = [
    "load_correct_tokenizer",
    "fix_sentencepiece_tokenizer",
    "check_tokenizer",
    "add_new_tokens",
    "fix_sentencepiece_gguf",
]
```

**Key Functions:**
- `try_fix_tokenizer()` - Fixes tokenizer configuration issues
- `convert_to_fast_tokenizer()` - Converts slow tokenizers to fast ones
- `load_correct_tokenizer()` - Loads and validates tokenizers
- `check_tokenizer()` - Validates tokenizer configuration

---

### **5. Import Organization**

**Main `__init__.py` imports (lines 261-269):**
```python
from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()
```

**Models `__init__.py` exports:**
```python
from .llama     import FastLlamaModel
from .loader    import FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel
from .mistral   import FastMistralModel
from .qwen2     import FastQwen2Model
from .qwen3     import FastQwen3Model
from .granite   import FastGraniteModel
# ... more model types
from .dpo       import PatchDPOTrainer, PatchKTOTrainer
from ._utils import is_bfloat16_supported, is_vLLM_available, __version__
```

---

### **6. Model-Specific Files Structure**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/`

**Files:**
- `loader.py` - Main loader with `FastLanguageModel` (the entry point)
- `llama.py` - Llama model optimizations (131KB, the largest)
- `_utils.py` - Shared utilities (68KB)
- `rl.py`, `rl_replacements.py` - RL training support
- `vision.py` - Vision model support
- `mistral.py`, `qwen2.py`, `qwen3.py`, `granite.py`, `gemma.py`, `gemma2.py`, `cohere.py` - Model-specific implementations

---

### **7. Registry System**

**Location:** `/unsloth/registry/`

**Key Components:**
- `registry.py` - Core registry implementation
- `_deepseek.py`, `_gemma.py`, `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_phi.py`, `_qwen.py` - Model registrations

**Registry Functions:**
```python
def register_models()           # Registers all models
def search_models(...)          # Search models by org, name, version, size, quant_types
```

---

### **8. Decorator Patterns & Key Patterns Used**

**Monkey-Patching with `types.MethodType()`:**
```python
import types
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
```

**Dynamic Function Creation (exec):**
- In `patch_saving_functions()`, the `push_to_hub` wrapper is created using `exec()` for dynamic signature handling

**Static Methods:**
- `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` - Used as factory method
- `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` - Used to apply LoRA

**Import Ordering Enforcement:**
- Lines 25-43 in main `__init__.py` warn if transformers/peft/trl are imported before unsloth
- This ensures optimizations are applied early

---

### **9. Where New Functionality Should Be Added**

Based on the structure:

1. **Model-specific behavior** → Add to corresponding file in `models/` (e.g., `llama.py`)
2. **Saving/export methods** → Add to `save.py`, then attach via `patch_saving_functions()`
3. **Tokenizer utilities** → Add to `tokenizer_utils.py`
4. **Training hooks** → Add to `trainer.py` or `models/rl.py`
5. **Model loading/configuration** → Modify `loader.py` or model-specific files
6. **Utility functions** → Add to `models/_utils.py` or `utils/`

---

### **10. File Paths Summary**

| Component | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Main package init | `/unsloth/__init__.py` |
| Save/Export functions | `/unsloth/save.py` |
| Tokenizer utilities | `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` |
| FastLanguageModel | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` |
| FastLlamaModel | `/unsloth/models/llama.py` |
| Model utilities | `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` |
| Registry system | `/unsloth/registry/registry.py` |
| Training patches | `/unsloth/trainer.py` |
| Vision support | `/unsloth/models/vision.py` |

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## LoRA Adapter Structure in Unsloth - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed understanding of how LoRA adapters are structured and how weights are accessed:

### 1. **LoRA Layer Definition & Architecture**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` and PEFT integration via `/unsloth/models/llama.py`

**Core Classes:**
- `LoRA_MLP` - Handles SwiGLU MLP projections with gate/up/down LoRA weights
- `LoRA_QKV` - Handles attention Q/K/V projections with LoRA weights
- `LoRA_W` - Generic single weight projection with LoRA

**Example from fast_lora.py (lines 28-91):**
```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    """
    ### LoRA weights
    G = G + Ag @ Bg  # Gate projection
    U = U + Au @ Bu  # Up projection
    W = W + Aw @ Bw  # Down projection
    """
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
                _forward_function, _backward_function,
                inplace = True,):
```

### 2. **Weight Matrix Storage & Access (A and B matrices)**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231)

The LoRA layers in PEFT use dictionaries indexed by adapter name. Access pattern:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale)"""
    
    # Get the active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA layers from dictionaries
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Returns a Linear layer module
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]  # Returns a Linear layer module
    
    # Extract the actual weight tensors
    A = lora_A_linear.weight  # Tensor of shape (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight  # Tensor of shape (out_features, r)
    
    return (
        W,                              # Base layer weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None), # Quantization state (for 4-bit)
        A,                              # LoRA A matrix
        B,                              # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # Scaling factor (alpha/r)
    )
```

**Key Attributes on PEFT Linear layers:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter_name → Linear(in_features, r)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter_name → Linear(r, out_features, bias=False)
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling coefficient (typically lora_alpha / lora_rank)
- `proj.active_adapters` or `proj.active_adapter` - Current active adapter name(s)

### 3. **LoRA Metadata & Configuration**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2266-2350)

**Stored in `model.peft_config["default"]`:**
```python
LoraConfig(
    r = 16,                           # LoRA rank
    lora_alpha = 16,                  # Scaling (typically same as r)
    target_modules = [                # Which layers to apply LoRA
        "q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
        "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"
    ],
    lora_dropout = 0.0,               # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    bias = "none",                    # Whether to use bias
    use_rslora = False,               # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    init_lora_weights = True,         # Initialize weights
    modules_to_save = None,           # Modules that don't use LoRA but are trainable
    layers_to_transform = None,       # Specific layers to transform (None = all)
    layers_pattern = None,            # Pattern matching for layers
    loftq_config = {},                # LoFTQ initialization config
)
```

**Accessing metadata:**
```python
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
r = peft_config["r"]
lora_alpha = peft_config["lora_alpha"]
target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
```

### 4. **LoRA Forward Computation**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 734-757)

```python
def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
    """
    Compute: output = X @ W + s * (X @ A) @ B
    where A, B are LoRA weights and s is the scaling factor
    """
    dtype = X.dtype
    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
    
    # Reshape if needed
    if X.dim() == 3:
        batch, seq_len, d = X.shape
        X = X.view(-1, X.shape[-1])
        reshape = True
    
    # Base weight computation
    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
    
    # LoRA contribution: s * (X @ A.T) @ B.T
    if A is not None:
        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)  # out += s * XA @ B
    
    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out
```

### 5. **State Dict Structure**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py` and PEFT integration

**Pattern in saved models:**
```
model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight  # Shape: (r, in_dim)
model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_B.default.weight  # Shape: (out_dim, r)
model.layers.0.self_attn.k_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.k_proj.lora_B.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.v_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.v_proj.lora_B.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.o_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.mlp.gate_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.mlp.up_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.mlp.down_proj.lora_A.default.weight
```

**Accessing from state dict:**
```python
state_dict = model.state_dict()
# Filter LoRA weights
lora_weights = {k: v for k, v in state_dict.items() if "lora_A" in k or "lora_B" in k}
```

### 6. **Accessing LoRA Weights at Runtime**

**From test utilities** (`/tests/utils/data_utils.py`, line 134):
```python
def get_peft_weights(model):
```

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## LoRA Adapter Structure in Unsloth - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed understanding of how LoRA adapters are structured and how weights are accessed:

### 1. **LoRA Layer Definition & Architecture**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` and PEFT integration via `/unsloth/models/llama.py`

**Core Classes:**
- `LoRA_MLP` - Handles SwiGLU MLP projections with gate/up/down LoRA weights
- `LoRA_QKV` - Handles attention Q/K/V projections with LoRA weights
- `LoRA_W` - Generic single weight projection with LoRA

**Example from fast_lora.py (lines 28-91):**
```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    """
    ### LoRA weights
    G = G + Ag @ Bg  # Gate projection
    U = U + Au @ Bu  # Up projection
    W = W + Aw @ Bw  # Down projection
    """
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
                _forward_function, _backward_function,
                inplace = True,):
```

### 2. **Weight Matrix Storage & Access (A and B matrices)**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-231)

The LoRA layers in PEFT use dictionaries indexed by adapter name. Access pattern:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale)"""
    
    # Get the active adapter name
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: 
        adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    # Access LoRA layers from dictionaries
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Returns a Linear layer module
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]  # Returns a Linear layer module
    
    # Extract the actual weight tensors
    A = lora_A_linear.weight  # Tensor of shape (r, in_features)
    B = lora_B_linear.weight  # Tensor of shape (out_features, r)
    
    return (
        W,                              # Base layer weight
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None), # Quantization state (for 4-bit)
        A,                              # LoRA A matrix
        B,                              # LoRA B matrix
        proj.scaling[adapter],          # Scaling factor (alpha/r)
    )
```

**Key Attributes on PEFT Linear layers:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter_name → Linear(in_features, r)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Dict mapping adapter_name → Linear(r, out_features, bias=False)
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling coefficient (typically lora_alpha / lora_rank)
- `proj.active_adapters` or `proj.active_adapter` - Current active adapter name(s)

### 3. **LoRA Metadata & Configuration**

**Location:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2266-2350)

**Stored in `model.peft_config["default"]`:**
```python
LoraConfig(
    r = 16,                           # LoRA rank
    lora_alpha = 16,                  # Scaling (typically same as r)
    target_modules = [                # Which layers to apply LoRA
        "q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
        "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"
    ],
    lora_dropout = 0.0,               # Dropout on LoRA inputs
    bias = "none",                    # Whether to use bias
    use_rslora = False,               # Rank-stabilized LoRA
    init_lora_weights = True,         # Initialize weights
    modules_to_save = None,           # Modules that don't use LoRA but are trainable
    layers_to_transform = None,       # Specific layers to transform (None = all)
    layers_pattern = None,            # Pattern matching for layers
    loftq_config = {},                # LoFTQ initialization config
)
```

**Accessing metadata:**
```python
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
r = peft_config["r"]
lora_alpha = peft_config["lora_alpha"]
target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
```

### 4. **LoRA Forward Computation**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 734-757)

```python
def matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, s, out = None):
    """
    Compute: output = X @ W + s * (X @ A) @ B
    where A, B are LoRA weights and s is the scaling factor
    """
    dtype = X.dtype
    W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
    
    # Reshape if needed
    if X.dim() == 3:
        batch, seq_len, d = X.shape
        X = X.view(-1, X.shape[-1])
        reshape = True
    
    # Base weight computation
    out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
    
    # LoRA contribution: s * (X @ A.T) @ B.T
    if A is not None:
        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
        XA = torch_matmul(X, A.to(dtype))
        out.addmm_(XA, B.to(dtype), alpha = s)  # out += s * XA @ B
    
    return out.view(batch, seq_len, -1) if reshape else out
```

### 5. **State Dict Structure**

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py` and PEFT integration

**Pattern in saved models:**
```
model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_A.default.weight  # Shape: (r, in_dim)
model.layers.0.self_attn.q_proj.lora_B.default.weight  # Shape: (out_dim, r)
model.layers.0.self_attn.k_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.k_proj.lora_B.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.v_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.v_proj.lora_B.default.weight
model.layers.0.self_attn.o_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.mlp.gate_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.mlp.up_proj.lora_A.default.weight
model.layers.0.mlp.down_proj.lora_A.default.weight
```

**Accessing from state dict:**
```python
state_dict = model.state_dict()
# Filter LoRA weights
lora_weights = {k: v for k, v in state_dict.items() if "lora_A" in k or "lora_B" in k}
```

### 6. **Accessing LoRA Weights at Runtime**

```python
    }

# Usage
for name, param in get_peft_weights(model).items():
    print(f"{name}: shape={param.shape}, dtype={param.dtype}")
```

### 7. **LoRA with Quantization Support**

**Location:** `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 200-223)

The unsloth implementation supports QAT (Quantization Aware Training):
```python
# Optional fake quantization for LoRA weights
lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)

if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
    A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
    B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
```

### 8. **ONNX & Export Status**

**No dedicated ONNX files found in the codebase.** The repository focuses on:
- PyTorch native serialization (`.bin`, `.safetensors`)
- GGUF export for llama.cpp compatibility (`save_to_gguf`)
- Merged model exports (combining LoRA into base weights)

### 9. **Key Files Reference**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | LoRA custom autograd functions |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | Weight access utilities (get_lora_parameters, matmul_lora) |

This structure allows unsloth to efficiently train LoRA adapters with optimized CUDA kernels while maintaining compatibility with the HuggingFace PEFT library's conventions.

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report summarizing all the findings:

## Comprehensive Analysis of Unsloth Save and Export Methods

Based on my thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here's a detailed breakdown of how save and export methods work:

### 1. **Directory Structure**

The main file organization:
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260750767-vj84yl/unsloth/save.py` - Core save/export implementation (2678 lines)
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260750767-vj84yl/unsloth/models/` - Model-specific patches
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775260750767-vj84yl/tests/saving/` - Tests for save functionality

### 2. **Main Save/Export Functions**

**Core Functions Defined in save.py:**

1. **`unsloth_save_model()`** (line 205-760) - Main orchestrator
   - Accepts parameters: `save_directory`, `save_method` (lora/merged_16bit/merged_4bit)
   - Handles LoRA merging into different formats
   - Manages memory efficiently with disk swapping for large models
   - Key parameters:
     - `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", or "merged_4bit"
     - `push_to_hub`: Boolean for HuggingFace Hub upload
     - `token`: HuggingFace authentication token
     - `max_shard_size`: Default "5GB" for model sharding
     - `safe_serialization`: Boolean for safetensors format
     - `temporary_location`: Disk location for memory overflow
     - `maximum_memory_usage`: RAM usage limit (0-0.95)

2. **GGUF Export Functions:**

   a. **`save_to_gguf()`** (line 952-1320) - Generic GGUF conversion
      - Converts models to GGUF format with multiple quantization options
      - Installs/uses llama.cpp for conversion
      - Quantization methods supported: q2_k, q3_k_m, q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q6_k, q8_0, f16, f32, etc.
      - Parameters:
        - `model_type`: Model architecture type
        - `model_dtype`: "float16" or "bfloat16"
        - `quantization_method`: List of quantization options
        - `first_conversion`: Initial dtype before quantization
        - `model_directory`: Where to save

   b. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`** (line 1768-1948)
      - Wrapper for GGUF saving as model method
      - Handles tokenizer integration
      - Creates Ollama Modelfile
      - Merges LoRA to 16bit first, then converts to GGUF
      - Parameters same as save_to_gguf plus:
        - `tokenizer`: Required for GGUF conversion
        - `push_to_hub`: Upload to HuggingFace Hub

   c. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()`** (line 1951-2117)
      - Same as save_pretrained_gguf but directly pushes to Hub
      - Takes `repo_id` instead of `save_directory`

3. **Merged Model Saving:**

   a. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 1321-1361)
      - Wrapper that calls unsloth_save_model with merge options
      - Supports: 16bit, 4bit, or LoRA-only saving

   b. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 1364-1407)
      - Same as merged save but pushes to Hub

4. **LoRA/Adapter Conversion:**

   a. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`** (line 2211-2262)
      - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
      - Uses llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py script
      - Saves locally

   b. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (line 2143-2209)
      - Same as above but uploads to Hub

5. **Generic/Vision Model Support:**

   a. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 2413-2453)
   b. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 2456-2499)
   - Handles non-standard architectures (vision models, etc.)

6. **TorchAO Quantization:**

   a. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (line 2502-2561)
      - Quantizes with PyTorch's torchao library
      - Merges LoRA first, then applies torchao quantization
      - Saves to directory + "-torchao" suffix

### 3. **Method Patching/Registration System**

**Location:** `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569-2678)

This is the critical function that dynamically adds methods to model objects:

```python
# Methods attached to model instances via types.MethodType:
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**How patching works:**
- Called automatically by model loaders (llama.py, vision.py, etc.)
- Uses Python's `types.MethodType()` to bind functions as instance methods
- Preserves original `push_to_hub()` as `original_push_to_hub`
- Wraps it to add Unsloth branding and optimizations

**Call locations:**
- `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2204, 2902)
- `unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964)
- `unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 2309)

### 4. **LoRA Weight Merging Process**

**Key function:** `_merge_lora()` (line 159-186)

```python
# Extracts LoRA parameters from layer
W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)

# Dequantizes if needed
if quant_state is not None:
    W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)

# Converts to float32 for merging
W = W.to(torch.float32).t()

# Applies LoRA: W = W + A * B (scaled by s)
W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)

# Converts back to original dtype
W = W.t().to(dtype)
```

**Special handling for different quantized layers:**
- Handles `bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit` (BnB 4-bit)
- Handles `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` (PEFT 4-bit)
- Handles `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` (PEFT regular)

### 5. **Model Layer Processing**

When saving merged 16bit models, iterates through:

```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)
```

### 6. **Memory Management Features**

The save system includes sophisticated memory handling:

- **RAM/VRAM Swapping:** If model doesn't fit in VRAM, saves to disk
- **Disk Temporary Storage:** Uses `_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers` directory
- **Memory Calculation:** Respects `maximum_memory_usage` (0-95% of system RAM)
- **CPU Count Detection:** Uses parallel pickle saving for low-CPU systems
- **Sharding Support:** Splits large models into 5GB chunks (configurable)
- **Safe Serialization:** Defaults to safetensors format for security

### 7. **HuggingFace Hub Integration**

**Functions:**
- `upload_to_huggingface()` (line 1495-1582)
- `create_huggingface_repo()` (line 1456-1492)
- `_determine_username()` (line 1436-1453)

**Features:**
- Auto-creates model card
- Uploads tensorboard files
- Handles organization repos (not just personal)
- Adds Unsloth branding to all uploads
- Sets "unsloth" tag automatically

### 8. **Supported Quantization Methods**

From `ALLOWED_QUANTS` dictionary (lines 79-107):

```
not_quantized  - Full precision
fast_quantized - q8_0 (recommended for speed)
quantized      - q4_k_m (recommended for compression)
f32, f16, bf16 - Full precision variants
q2_k to q6_k   - Varying compression levels
iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs - Ultra-compressed (not fully supported)
q3_k_xs        - 3-bit extra small
```

### 9. **Export Methods Summary**

| Method | Purpose | Output | Parameters |
|--------|---------|--------|------------|
| `save_pretrained_merged` | Save merged model | Float16/4bit weights | save_method, tokenizer |
| `push_to_hub_merged` | Upload merged model | HF Hub repo | repo_id, tokenizer |
| `save_pretrained_gguf` | Save as GGUF format | .gguf files | quantization_method |
| `push_to_hub_gguf` | Upload GGUF files | HF Hub repo | repo_id, quantization_method |
| `save_pretrained_ggml` | Save LoRA as GGML | ggml-adapter-model.bin | (none additional) |
| `push_to_hub_ggml` | Upload LoRA GGML | HF Hub repo | repo_id |
| `save_pretrained_torchao` | Save with torchao quantization | Quantized weights | torchao_config |

### 10. **Usage Example from Tests**

```python
# Save merged 16bit model
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Save as GGUF with quantization
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"
)

# Push GGUF to Hub
model.push_to_hub_gguf(
    repo_id="username/model-name",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="fast_quantized"
)

# Save with TorchAO quantization
model.save_pretrained_torchao(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    torchao_config=Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
)
```

This comprehensive system provides multiple export pathways optimized for different deployment scenarios - from full-precision HF Hub uploads to highly-compressed GGUF files for local inference via llama.cpp.

   a. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 1321-1361)
      - Wrapper that calls unsloth_save_model with merge options
      - Supports: 16bit, 4bit, or LoRA-only saving

   b. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 1364-1407)
      - Same as merged save but pushes to Hub

4. **LoRA/Adapter Conversion:**

   a. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`** (line 2211-2262)
      - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
      - Uses llama.cpp's convert-lora-to-ggml.py script
      - Saves locally

   b. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (line 2143-2209)
      - Same as above but uploads to Hub

5. **Generic/Vision Model Support:**

   a. **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`** (line 2413-2453)
   b. **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`** (line 2456-2499)
   - Handles non-standard architectures (vision models, etc.)

6. **TorchAO Quantization:**

   a. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (line 2502-2561)
      - Quantizes with PyTorch's torchao library
      - Merges LoRA first, then applies torchao quantization
      - Saves to directory + "-torchao" suffix

### 3. **Method Patching/Registration System**

**Location:** `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2569-2678)

This is the critical function that dynamically adds methods to model objects:

```python
# Methods attached to model instances via types.MethodType:
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
```

**How patching works:**
- Called automatically by model loaders (llama.py, vision.py, etc.)
- Uses Python's `types.MethodType()` to bind functions as instance methods
- Preserves original `push_to_hub()` as `original_push_to_hub`
- Wraps it to add Unsloth branding and optimizations

**Call locations:**
- `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2204, 2902)
- `unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 703, 918, 964)
- `unsloth/chat_templates.py` (line 2309)

### 4. **LoRA Weight Merging Process**

**Key function:** `_merge_lora()` (line 159-186)

```python
# Extracts LoRA parameters from layer
W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)

# Dequantizes if needed
if quant_state is not None:
    W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)

# Converts to float32 for merging
W = W.to(torch.float32).t()

# Applies LoRA: W = W + A * B (scaled by s)
W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)

# Converts back to original dtype
W = W.t().to(dtype)
```

**Special handling for different quantized layers:**
- Handles `bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit` (BnB 4-bit)
- Handles `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` (PEFT 4-bit)
- Handles `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` (PEFT regular)

### 5. **Model Layer Processing**

When saving merged 16bit models, iterates through:

```python
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)

LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)
```

### 6. **Memory Management Features**

The save system includes sophisticated memory handling:

- **RAM/VRAM Swapping:** If model doesn't fit in VRAM, saves to disk
- **Disk Temporary Storage:** Uses `_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers` directory
- **Memory Calculation:** Respects `maximum_memory_usage` (0-95% of system RAM)
- **CPU Count Detection:** Uses parallel pickle saving for low-CPU systems
- **Sharding Support:** Splits large models into 5GB chunks (configurable)
- **Safe Serialization:** Defaults to safetensors format for security

### 7. **HuggingFace Hub Integration**

**Functions:**
- `upload_to_huggingface()` (line 1495-1582)
- `create_huggingface_repo()` (line 1456-1492)
- `_determine_username()` (line 1436-1453)

**Features:**
- Auto-creates model card
- Uploads tensorboard files
- Handles organization repos (not just personal)
- Adds Unsloth branding to all uploads
- Sets "unsloth" tag automatically

### 8. **Supported Quantization Methods**

From `ALLOWED_QUANTS` dictionary (lines 79-107):

```
not_quantized  - Full precision
fast_quantized - q8_0 (recommended for speed)
quantized      - q4_k_m (recommended for compression)
f32, f16, bf16 - Full precision variants
q2_k to q6_k   - Varying compression levels
iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs - Ultra-compressed (not fully supported)
q3_k_xs        - 3-bit extra small
```

### 9. **Export Methods Summary**

| Method | Purpose | Output | Parameters |
|--------|---------|--------|------------|
| `save_pretrained_merged` | Save merged model | Float16/4bit weights | save_method, tokenizer |
| `push_to_hub_merged` | Upload merged model | HF Hub repo | repo_id, tokenizer |
| `save_pretrained_gguf` | Save as GGUF format | .gguf files | quantization_method |
| `push_to_hub_gguf` | Upload GGUF files | HF Hub repo | repo_id, quantization_method |
| `save_pretrained_ggml` | Save LoRA as GGML | ggml-adapter-model.bin | (none additional) |
| `push_to_hub_ggml` | Upload LoRA GGML | HF Hub repo | repo_id |
| `save_pretrained_torchao` | Save with torchao quantization | Quantized weights | torchao_config |

### 10. **Usage Example from Tests**

```python
# Save merged 16bit model
model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    save_method="merged_16bit"
)

# Save as GGUF with quantization
model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="q4_k_m"
)

# Push GGUF to Hub
model.push_to_hub_gguf(
    repo_id="username/model-name",
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    quantization_method="fast_quantized"
)

# Save with TorchAO quantization
model.save_pretrained_torchao(
    save_path,
    tokenizer=tokenizer,
    torchao_config=Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
)
```

This comprehensive system provides multiple export pathways optimized for different deployment scenarios - from full-precision HF Hub uploads to highly-compressed GGUF files for local inference via llama.cpp.

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass
